description: Boot power-on sequence for M1 Pro/Max. Adapted from M1 reference. Key difference from A2681: Viper VR and Monaco VR must power up before SoC dynamic domains are ready.

sequence.name: USB-C Cable Insertion — 5V Default
sequence.action: USB-C cable inserted. Charger provides default 5V. CD3217 (UF400/UF500/UG400) internal MOSFET passes 5V.

sequence.name: Charger Input Active — PPDCIN_AON
sequence.action: Charger input path enabled. Multi-FET gate drive active. CHGR_AUX_OK asserted.

sequence.name: PPBUS_AON Established
sequence.action: Main power bus comes up from battery (12.6V) or adapter input.
sequence.test_point: CR765 area (Saf: PPBUS_AON short at 5V/0.012A → CR765 cap)

sequence.name: PP3V8_AON Powers Up — U5700 SVR AON
sequence.action: U5700 converts PPBUS_AON → PP3V8_AON (3.8V, 30A max). Multi-phase output. Feeds both PMUs + WiFi + display + USB-C.

sequence.name: SPMU & MPMU Startup — AON and S2 Rails Cascade
sequence.action: Both PMUs receive PP3V8_AON and generate output rails in hardware-defined sequence. AON rails first, then S2, then S1.

sequence.name: Standalone VRs Power Up — 5V and 3V3
sequence.action: TPS62130 generates PP5V_S2, separate VR generates PP3V3_S2. Load switches enabled for USB-C ports and HDMI.

sequence.name: Viper VR Powers Up — PCPU Domains
sequence.action: U9300 Viper VR controller starts multi-phase power stages. PCPU clusters receive DVFS voltage. NOT present on A2681.

sequence.name: Monaco VR Powers Up — GPU/Fabric Domains
sequence.action: Monaco VR instances start. 10 bucks per instance generate GPU, fabric, and SoC compute domains. NOT present on A2681.

sequence.name: M1 Pro/Max Hard Start Complete — SoC Commands CD3217
sequence.action: SoC completed OTP and hard start. Commands CD3217 to negotiate PD.

sequence.name: PD Negotiation → 20V Boost
sequence.action: CD3217 negotiates 20V from charger. PPVBUS transitions 5V → 20V. Protection isolation passes. PPBUS_AON stabilises.

sequence.name: Soft Start — SSD, BIOS, Display
sequence.action: SoC reads dual SSDs (SSD0 + SSD1 via Ocarina), loads firmware, display power sequencer (page 111) enables Mini-LED Liquid Retina XDR panel, Luxe backlight driver activates. Apple LOGO appears.
subsystems: HDMI (Madea, page 89) — if external display connected
